note:
随着越来越多的团队被投入到远程工作中，我花了几周的时间与一些比任何人都更了解这个主题的人进行了交谈：创始人和高管，他们花费了无数的时间让远程工作成为…。工作。
其中一些公司，如GitLab、Doist、Zapier、Matterost、FlexJobs和YouNeedABudget，从最早的时候起就一直在完全远程工作。其他人，比如Twilio，多年来一直处于部分偏远的状态，但发现自己改变了做法，将远程工作扩大到了一个近3000人的团队。
在找亮点吗？我已经把他们的一些小贴士拿出来，整理在下面，以便阅读。想要完整的采访吗？您可以在此处找到这些内容：
这可能是我从所有这些团队中听到最多的一件事：写作。一些事情。趴下。让它成为一种习惯。
概述您的公司价值。记录决策-以及决策是如何做出的。做会议笔记。公开跟踪项目进度，并保持路线图透明。
note_en:
With more and more teams being thrown headfirst into remote work, I spent a few weeks talking with some of the people who know the topic better than just about anyone: the founders and execs who’ve put countless hours into making remote work … work.
 Some of these companies — like GitLab, Doist, Zapier, Mattermost, FlexJobs and YouNeedABudget — have been working fully remote since their earliest days. Others, like Twilio, had been partly remote for years but found themselves shifting gears to expand remote work to a team of nearly three thousand people.
 Looking for the highlights? I’ve pulled out some of their tips and collated them below for easy reading. Want the full interviews? You can find those here:
   It was probably the thing I heard most from all of these teams: Write. Things. Down. Make it a habit.
 Outline your company values. Document decisions — and how decisions are made. Take meeting notes. Track project progress publicly, and keep roadmaps transparent.
title: 如何从最了解远程工作的团队那里做好远程工作
title_en: How to do remote work right, from the teams that know it best

亚马逊警告其无线耳机可能存在过热的风险，并敦促客户更新他们的软件，以确保它们的安全。
周三，客户收到一封电子邮件，称在“非常罕见的情况下”，Echo Buds在他们的充电盒中可能会过热。
亚马逊表示，出于高度谨慎，它已经发布了软件更新来解决这个问题。
亚马逊不愿透露这是否意味着耳机可能起火或爆炸，但确实透露，调查是由一名忧心忡忡的客户联系上的。
亚马逊表示，当Echo Buds通过蓝牙连接到手机和Alexa应用程序时，车主将自动收到更新。
它还说，如果回声芽是一份礼物，顾客应该立即通知收件人。
这种耳塞在亚马逊网站上仍在出售，但在8月下旬之前都标有缺货标签。
Android Central技术博客的执行主编丹尼尔·巴德(Daniel Bader)分享了这封电子邮件的细节。
电子邮件中写道：“我们写信是为了通知你，你的Echo Buds有一个重要的软件更新。”
“我们最近确定，在非常罕见的情况下，回声芽有可能在充电时过热。
电子邮件还说，同样的软件更新提高了Echo Buds电池的长期性能。
许多品牌的无线耳塞在盒子里都有自己的电池，当耳塞放在里面时，它会自动充电。
电池热量通常在充电时由设备释放，但通常保持在严格的安全限制内。
亚马逊没有说明过热问题是由过度充电还是电池盒内的任何其他具体问题造成的。
note_en:
Amazon has warned its wireless earphones may be at risk of overheating and is urging customers to update their software, to make them safe.
 Customers were sent an email on Wednesday, saying in “very rare cases” Echo Buds could overheat in their charging case.
 Amazon said it had released a software update to fix the issue "out of an abundance of caution".
  Amazon would not say if this meant the earphones could catch fire or explode, but did disclose that its investigation had been prompted by a concerned customer getting in touch.
  Amazon said owners would automatically receive the update when the Echo Buds were connected via Bluetooth to a mobile phone and the Alexa app.
 It also said if the Echo Buds were a gift, the customer should alert the recipient immediately.
 The earbuds are still for sale on the Amazon site but labelled as "out of stock" until late August.
  The details of the email were shared by Daniel Bader, managing editor at the Android Central technology blog.
 “We are writing to inform you about an important software update for your Echo Buds,” the email said.
 “We recently determined that in very rare cases it is possible for Echo Buds to overheat while in the charging case."
 The email also said the same software update "improves the long-term performance of Echo Buds' batteries".
 Many brands of wireless earbuds have their own battery inside the case, which automatically charges the earbuds when they are placed inside.
 Battery heat is often given out by devices while charging but usually stays within strict safety limits.
 Amazon did not say if the heat problem was caused by overcharging or any other specific issue within the battery case.
title: 亚马逊警告Alexa Echo Buds耳机存在过热安全风险
title_en: Amazon warns Alexa Echo Buds earphones pose overheating safety risk

note:
在美国对8.7万多名新车买家和租户进行的调查中，电动汽车公司特斯拉(Tesla)的可靠性排名最后。
J.D.Power US Initial Quality研究评估了拥有特斯拉后的前三个月遇到的问题，其中包括2020年首次使用特斯拉。然而，它与其他品牌是分开的，因为据报道，这家由埃隆·马斯克(Elon Musk)创立的公司不允许其客户在美国50个州中的15个州接受调查，这意味着它不符合研究的排名资格标准。
然而，在接受调查的35个州中，特斯拉车主的表现不佳。这项研究根据每百辆汽车的问题数量(PP100)对汽车进行评级，这意味着汽车制造商的得分越低，其汽车的初始制造质量就越好(根据其客户的说法)。
道奇和起亚取得了最好的成绩，每100辆车有136个问题-没有一个品牌的得分低于100分。紧随其后的主要是美国品牌：雪佛兰和公羊(141)，创世纪(142)，三菱(148)，别克(150)和通用汽车(151)。排在前十名的是欧洲人更熟悉的汽车制造商：大众(152)和现代(153)。
根据JD Power的结果，受英国人欢迎的高端品牌表现不佳，远低于166个PP100的行业平均水平。捷豹(Jaguar)、梅赛德斯(Mercedes)、沃尔沃(Volvo)、奥迪(Audi)和路虎(Land Rover)在头三个月的PP100都超过了190，使它们处于主图表的最底部。然而，特斯拉的得分远远低于路虎：其汽车的PP100被发现为250。
这一结果可能会令人惊讶，原因有几个，尤其是对美国品牌的明显偏见。美国汽车制造商的特定车型也表现出色：雪佛兰Sonic被选为最佳小型车；雪佛兰Malibu被选为最佳中型车；福特野马(Ford Mustang)被选为最佳中型运动型车；凯迪拉克CT5被选为最佳中型豪华车；凯迪拉克CT6被选为最佳中上豪华车。美国汽车在11个类别中的5个类别中获胜，Sonic被发现是总体上最高的初始质量车型。
因此，美国人并不急于将本土品牌抛诸脑后，这或许让总部位于加州的特斯拉表现如此之差变得更加令人惊讶。
它的客户也经常被认为非常忠诚，特斯拉车主俱乐部(Tesla Owners Club UK)Facebook组的成员经常为有问题的司机提供实事求是的建议。今天早上，有一条帖子写道：“我试过复位，但按下…按钮后，仪表板就打不开了。有什么主意吗？“。其中一人回答说，这辆车是否处于代客模式。当被告知不是时，他们紧接着说：“好的。这是随机手套锁的“正常”原因。试着把它戴上，然后把它关掉？“。另一位好心的撒玛利亚人写道：“里面有什么吗？有时，由于门上有一点重量或向下的压力，门闩不够人性化，无法松开。试着在按Release…(释放按钮)的同时向上推车门。“
JD Power的结果也令人惊讶，因为特斯拉在大西洋彼岸的一些可靠性测试中表现良好。当路透社Driving.co.uk联系到特斯拉时，该公司的一位发言人对JD Power的调查不予置评，但他指出，特斯拉在What Car中排名第四？可靠性调查2019，得分96.9%，仅次于雷克萨斯、丰田和铃木。
然而，美国的研究结果确实证实了有关建筑质量问题的坊间报道。尽管特斯拉Model Y不会被包括在JD Power的调查中，因为交付只是在调查即将结束时才开始，但特斯拉Model Y因特殊问题而受到强调。一些美国客户报告说，汽车的后备箱盖无法合上，后座没有正确连接到车架上。也有投诉相对较小的油漆和装饰问题。
本月初，特斯拉首席执行官埃隆·马斯克给公司员工发了一封电子邮件，提到了Model Y的质量控制问题。在这封电子邮件中，他表示：“对我们来说，提高Model Y的产量并将整改需求降至最低是极其重要的。”我想让你知道，现在对特斯拉来说真的很不一样。“。
更新：本文的前一个版本建议与型号Y有关的问题可能是导致JD Power结果的原因之一。特斯拉联系我们说，J.D.Power 2020美国初始质量研究是在2019年2月至2020年3月底之间进行的，直到3月份的最后一周才开始交付Model Y。我们很高兴在上面澄清这一点。
note_en:
ELECTRIC car company Tesla has ranked last for reliability in American survey of more than 87,000 buyers and lessees of new cars.
 The J.D. Power US Initial Quality Study, which gauges problems encountered in the first three months of ownership, included Tesla for the first time in 2020. It was separated from the other brands, however, as the Elon Musk-founded firm reportedly wouldn’t allow its customers to be surveyed in 15 of America’s 50 states, meaning that it didn’t meet the study’s ranking eligibility criteria.
 However, in the 35 states where Tesla owners were surveyed, it performed poorly. The study rates cars in terms of the number of problems per hundred cars (PP100), meaning that the lower a car maker’s score, the better initial build quality its cars have (according to its customers).
 Dodge and Kia achieved the best results, with 136 problems per 100 cars — no marque scored less than 100. They were followed mostly by American brands: Chevrolet and Ram (141), Genesis (142), Mitsubishi (148), Buick (150) and GMC (151). The top 10 was rounded out by car makers more familiar to Europeans: Volkswagen (152) and Hyundai (153).
 Premium brands popular with Brits performed badly, coming well below the industry average of 166 PP100, according to the JD Power results. Jaguar, Mercedes, Volvo, Audi and Land Rover all had more than 190 PP100 in their first three months, putting them at the very bottom of the main chart. However, Tesla’s score is far worse than Land Rover’s: its cars were found to have 250 PP100.
  The results may raise eyebrows for a couple of reasons, not least the apparent bias for U.S. brands. Specific models from American car makers also did very well: the Chevrolet Sonic was voted the best small car; Chevrolet Malibu the best midsize car; Ford Mustang the best midsize sporty car; Cadillac CT5 the best midsize premium car; and Cadillac CT6 the best upper-midsize premium car. American cars won in five of the 11 categories and the Sonic was found to be the Highest Initial Quality Model overall.
 Americans are not, therefore, quick to throw home-grown brands under the bus, which perhaps makes it all the more surprising that California-based Tesla did so poorly.
 Its customers are often thought of as fiercely loyal, too, with members of the Tesla Owners Club UK Facebook group often offering matter-of-fact advice for drivers with issues. This morning, one post read: “I have tried a reset but the glove compartment will not open after pressing the button…any ideas?” One response asked if the car was in Valet mode. When told it wasn’t, they followed with: “ok. That’s the ‘normal’ cause of random glove locking. Try putting it on and turning it off?” Another good samaritan wrote: “Is there something in it? Sometimes with a bit of weight or downward pressure on the door the latch isn’t man enough to disengage. Try pushing up on the door whilst pressing the release…”
 The JD Power results are also surprising in that Tesla has performed well in some reliability tests this side of the Atlantic. When approached by  Driving.co.uk, a spokesperson had no comment on the JD Power survey but pointed out that Tesla came fourth in the  What Car? Reliability Survey 2019, with a score of 96.9%, behind only Lexus, Toyota and Suzuki.
 However, the results of the US study do corroborate anecdotal reports of build quality issues. Although it would not have been included in the JD Power survey, as deliveries only began just as the survey was closing, the Tesla Model Y has been highlighted for particular problems. Some American customers reported cars with boot lids that wouldn’t close and in which the back seats were not attached properly to the frame. There were also complaints of comparatively minor paint and trim issues.
 Tesla’s CEO Elon Musk sent an email to company employees at the beginning of this month which referred to the Model Y’s quality control issues. In the email he said: “It is extremely important for us to ramp up Model Y production and minimise rectification needs. I want you to know that it really makes a difference to Tesla right now.”
 Update: A previous version of this article suggested issues with the Model Y might have contributed to the JD Power results. Tesla contacted us to say the J.D. Power 2020 US Initial Quality Study was conducted between February 2019 and the end of March 2020, and Model Y deliveries didn’t start until the last week in March. We’re happy to clarify this above.
title: 特斯拉在美国司机调查中可靠性最差
title_en: Tesla Worst for Reliability in American Driver Survey

看起来Gmail很快就会发生巨大的变化。推特用户塔欣·拉赫曼(Tahin Rahman)发布了泄露的幻灯片(9to5Google最先发现)，详细描述了Gmail、Google Docs、Google Chat和Google Meet之间的合并，看起来很快就会出现在网络和移动设备上。谷歌“下一代云2020”大会昨天拉开帷幕，并将在接下来的三周内举行，我们过去听到过关于这件事的详细传言，所以幻灯片似乎是很早就泄露出来的。(这句话的意思是：“谷歌下一个2020年的云大会”昨天拉开帷幕，并将持续三周时间，我们在过去也听到过关于这件事的传言)。
所有这一切的目标似乎是将Gmail转变为一个一站式的生产力网站，在这里你可以进行松弛风格的基于房间的聊天或单人聊天，进行视频通话，编辑文档和发送电子邮件。桌面网站在顶部页眉和侧边栏获得了额外的控件，而主面板-通常显示收件箱或消息-看起来可以换成其他内容，比如Google Doc。会议视频呼叫可以是全屏的，也可以在画中画风格的窗口中浮动。别忘了，这一切都是在2018年重新设计时引入的右侧面板之外的，它还可以让你在Gmail中打开谷歌日历、Keep和Tasks。有了这种设计，它就像是把谷歌的所有生产力应用--Gmail、聊天、会议、日历、Keep和任务--都塞进了一个页面，让你想知道为什么它还叫Gmail。
Gmail有一个并排的双面板视图已经有一段时间了，左边是Outlook风格的收件箱，右边是邮件。经过这次重新设计，看起来更多的注意力集中在双面板视图上。默认情况下，聊天页面使用此双面板视图，您可以在左侧面板中显示&#34；Chat，&#34；Files，&#34；或&#34；Tasks&#34；，在右侧面板中显示文档或其他内容。谷歌似乎正在采用Gmail的布局，并将其用于各种其他功能。
移动Gmail应用程序也在进行改进，在单一的Gmail应用程序中增加了聊天室、聊天室和碰头会的底部选项卡，所有这些都是在Gmail应用程序中进行的。在Gmail应用程序中，所有这两个选项卡都包含在&#34；Mail，&#34；Chat，&#34；Roomes，&#34；和&#34；Meet&#34；中。
由于Gmail是谷歌最受欢迎和最具粘性的产品之一(这意味着人们在上面花费了大量时间)，它经常被用作不太受欢迎的谷歌产品的特洛伊木马。在这种情况下，除了所有的生产力功能，Gmail还被用来提升Google Meet(该公司的Zoom竞争对手)和Google Chat(该公司的Slake克隆，前身是HangoutsChat)。这两款应用都是谷歌需要一遍又一遍地不断拆卸和重建其即时通讯应用的受害者，这使得它很难建立任何形式的用户基础。聊天和会议都是相当新的、不成熟的应用程序，它们还没有准备好赶上新冠肺炎带来的在家工作视频聊天的热潮。
在此之前，我们已经看到Gmail被Google+和Google Buzz所感染，那是在Google使用Gmail追逐最后的互联网时尚--社交媒体的时候。生产力的增加让Gmail看起来更加复杂，但至少它们符合某种合理的工作流程。
在接下来的三周里，我们可能会在Google Cloud Next上听到更多关于这方面的消息。7月21日开始了生产力与协作周，这听起来是正式宣布这些变化的好时机。一场名为“G Suite中的沟通：Gmail、聊天、会议等的未来”的演讲听起来很值得一看。
note_en:
It looks like big changes are coming to Gmail soon. Twitter user Tahin Rahman posted  leaked slides (first spotted by  9to5Google) detailing a merger between Gmail, Google Docs, Google Chat, and Google Meet that looks to be coming to the Web and mobile soon. Google's " Cloud Next 2020" conference kicked off yesterday and will be ongoing for the next three weeks, and we've  heard rumors in the past detailing this exact thing, so the slides appear to have just gotten leaked early.
 The goal of all this looks to be turning Gmail into a one-stop-shop productivity site, where you can do Slack-style room-based chat or single chats, make video calls, edit documents, and send emails. The desktop site is getting extra controls in the top header and sidebar, while the main panel—which normally shows the inbox or a message—looks like it can be swapped out for other content, like a Google Doc. Meet video calls can be full-screened or float around in a picture-in-picture-style window. Don't forget, this is all  in addition to the right-side panel that was introduced in the  2018 redesign, which also lets you open Google Calendar, Keep, and Tasks inside Gmail. With this design, it's like having every Google productivity app—Gmail, Chat, Meet, Calendar, Keep, and Tasks—crammed into a single page that makes you wonder why it's even called "Gmail" anymore.
 Gmail has had a side-by-side two-panel view for a while, showing an Outlook-style inbox on the left and a message on the right. With this redesign, it looks like there's more of a focus on the two-panel view. The "Chats" page uses this two-panel view by default, and you can show "Chat," "Files," or "Tasks" in the left panel, with a document or something else living in the right panel. Google appears to be taking the layout of Gmail and using it for all sorts of other functionality.
 The mobile Gmail app is getting revamped, too, with bottom tabs for "Mail," "Chat," "Rooms," and "Meet" all in the single Gmail app.
  Since Gmail is one of Google's most popular and "sticky" products (meaning people spend a lot of time in it), it is often used as a trojan horse for less popular Google products. In this case, along with all the productivity stuff, Gmail is being used to boost Google Meet (the company's Zoom competitor) and Google Chat (the company's Slack clone, formerly Hangouts Chat). Both apps are victims of Google's need to constantly tear down and rebuild its messaging apps over and over again, making it hard to build any kind of user base. Chat and Meet are both pretty new and immature apps, and they weren't ready in time to ride the work-from-home video chat craze brought about by COVID-19.
 Previously, we've seen Gmail get  infected with Google+ and  Google Buzz, back when Google was using Gmail to chase the last Internet fad, social media. The productivity additions make Gmail look way more complicated, but at least they fit some kind of reasonable workflow.
 We'll probably hear more about this during Google Cloud Next, which is happening for the next three weeks. July 21 starts the " Productivity and collaboration" week, which sounds like a good spot to formally announce these changes. A talk called "Communication in G Suite: The future of Gmail, Chat, Meet, and More" sounds like the one to watch.
title: 即将到来的Gmail重新设计使其成为一站式生产力套件
title_en: Upcoming Gmail redesign turns it into a one-stop productivity suite

note:
根据最近的一项研究，现代猫的祖先跟随早期农民进入欧洲，但并不是宠物。来自新石器时代波兰的六只猫的骨骼中的氮同位素比率表明，这些古老的猫捕食以人类农民为食的啮齿类动物，但它们的饮食与当地人和他们信任的家犬并不完全相同。换句话说，猫的生活方式与现代郊狼相似。
所有现代猫的血统都可以追溯到近东野猫；事实上，根据它们的DNA来区分家猫和这些野猫仍然有点棘手。大约在5300年前的某个时候，这些野猫似乎注意到老鼠、田鼠和榛子松鸡等啮齿动物成群结队地来到人类聚居地吃庄稼和食物。啮齿类动物为获取谷物而来，而猫科动物则为容易、充足的猎物而来。
在4200到2300年前，一批来自中亚的早期农民迁入欧洲，在那里他们与已经生活在那里的狩猎-采集者进行了互动。一些野猫紧随其后；考古学家在波兰发现了大约同一时期的近东野猫骨架。尼古拉斯·哥白尼大学的考古学家Magdalena Krajcarz和她的同事说，这些猫并不是真的和人类一起旅行--它们只是在追赶猎物。(这个论点听起来确实有点像猫写的。)。
Krajcarz和她的同事检查了在波兰南部发现的六只近东野猫的骨头的化学成分。当营养在食物链上传递时，氮-15往往比其他稳定的氮同位素(氮-14)传递得更多，所以它们之间的比例可以暗示动物吃的是什么。国内作物也往往含有更丰富的氮-15，因为它是以用作肥料的粪便的形式添加的，所以高比例的氮-15也可能意味着饮食中含有丰富的国内作物-或者吃这些作物的动物的肉。
近东猫的氮-15含量相当高。但更重要的是，猫的水平与当地以农作物为食的啮齿类动物骨骼中的氮-15水平非常接近。这是一个分子版本的确凿证据，表明大约75%到95%的猫咪菜单由以农民农作物和食品店为食的啮齿动物组成。
但当Krajcarz和她的同事将这些猫与古代人和附近定居点(如Bronocice)的家犬进行比较时，他们发现人和他们的狗的氮-15水平甚至比猫更高。这表明，人们的饮食几乎完全以种植的农作物为基础，并与他们的狗狗同伴分享他们的食物。
另一方面，猫似乎生活在聚居地附近，并利用了一些与人类为邻的东西，比如可以接触到大量喂饱的老鼠。它们的氮15值略低，但仍然很高，这表明它们主要以农作物害虫为生，但也捕食其他种类的猎物。
生态学家称这种生活方式为共生，今天你可以在现代城市的狐狸、郊狼、浣熊和乌鸦身上看到这种生活方式。对猫来说，同体行为是通向驯化之路的一步--当然，按照它们的条件。人类住区中发现的最早的猫科动物遗骸可以追溯到3000年后的罗马时期的波兰，它们的氮-15水平与人类和狗的水平更接近。
当第一批近东野猫跟随农民进入欧洲时，他们发现自己与已经生活在那里的欧洲野猫瓜分了一个生态位。Krajcarz和她的同事们研究的欧洲野猫骨的氮-15水平与近东猫相似，但分布范围更广。这表明，虽然近东猫科动物主要捕猎农作物害虫，但欧洲猫科动物只是将以作物为食的啮齿类动物添加到它们当地的菜单中。也许这在一定程度上解释了为什么近东的猫，而不是欧洲的猫，最终被驯化了。
Krajcarz和她的同事们研究的所有六只近东猫都来自山上的洞穴，俯瞰着人们耕种的肥沃低地。它们似乎最终出现在洞穴里，要么是因为那里是它们生活和死亡的地方，要么是因为体型较大的捕食者把它们作为零食拖回了家。人们生活在高地，与下面山谷中的农业聚居地相比，他们以更小、更稀疏的群体生活。但是没有证据表明他们埋葬了这些猫。
这些洞穴距离山谷中附近的大型农业定居点30至45公里(19至28英里)。这暗示了这六只猫的某些特性，但它还有很多东西没有告诉我们。考古学家还没有在波兰的任何新石器时代聚居地发现猫的骨头，所以目前还没有证据告诉我们猫是在离人类更近的地方生活和狩猎，还是有些猫实际上生活在人类的房屋或食物储存结构中。
note_en:
The ancestors of modern cats followed early farmers into Europe but weren't pets, according to a recent study. Nitrogen isotope ratios in the bones of six cats from Neolithic Poland suggest that these ancient cats hunted rodents that ate human farmers' crops, but they didn't eat quite the same diet as local people and their trusty domestic dogs. In other words, the cats lived a lifestyle similar to modern coyotes.
  All modern cats trace their lineage back to Near Eastern wildcats; in fact, it's still a bit tricky to tell the domestic cats from these wildcats based on their DNA. Sometime around 5,300 years ago, it seems that these wild cats noticed that rodents like mice, voles, and hazel grouse flocked to human settlements to eat crops and food stores. The rodents came for the grain, and the cats came for the easy, abundant prey.
 Between 4,200 and 2,300 years ago, a population of early farmers from Central Asia moved into Europe, where they interacted with the hunter-gatherers who already lived there. Some wild cats tagged along; archaeologists found Near Eastern wildcat skeletons in Poland from around the same period. Archaeologist Magdalena Krajcarz of Nicolaus Copernicus University and her colleagues say the cats weren't really traveling  with the humans—they were just following their prey. (This argument does sound a bit like a cat wrote it.)
 Krajcarz and her colleagues examined the chemical makeup of bones from six Near Eastern wildcats found in southern Poland. As nutrients get passed up the food chain, nitrogen-15 tends to get passed along more than the other stable nitrogen isotope (nitrogen-14), so the ratio between them can suggest what an animal was eating. Domestic crops also tend to be richer in nitrogen-15, because it gets added in the form of manure used as fertilizer, so a high ratio of nitrogen-15 can also suggest a diet rich in domestic crops—or in meat from animals that ate those crops.
 The Near Eastern cats had fairly high levels of nitrogen-15. But what's more important is that the cats' levels lined up very closely with the nitrogen-15 levels in the bones of local crop-eating rodents. It was the molecular version of a smoking gun, suggesting that about 75 percent to 95 percent of the cats' menu consisted of rodents that fed on farmers' crops and food stores.
  But when Krajcarz and her colleagues compared the cats to ancient people and domestic dogs from nearby settlements, like Bronocice, they found that people and their dogs had even higher nitrogen-15 levels than cats. That suggests that people were eating a diet based almost entirely on farmed crops and were sharing their food with their canine companions.
 Cats, on the other hand, seem to have lived near settlements and taken advantage of some of the things that go along with having humans as neighbors, like access to plenty of well-fed mice. Their slightly lower—but still high—nitrogen-15 values suggest that they lived mostly on crop pests but also hunted other kinds of prey.
 Ecologists call this lifestyle synanthropy, and today you can see it in modern urban foxes, coyotes, raccoons, and crows. For cats, synanthropy was a step on the way to domestication—on their terms, of course. The earliest cat remains found in human settlements date to the Roman period in Poland, 3,000 years later, and their nitrogen-15 levels are much closer to humans and dogs.
 When the first Near Eastern wildcats followed farmers into Europe, they found themselves splitting an ecological niche with European wildcats that already lived there. The European wildcat bones Krajcarz and her colleagues looked at had nitrogen-15 levels similar to the Near Eastern cats but spread across a wider range. That suggests that, while the Near Eastern cats mostly hunted crop pests, the European cats just added the crop-eating rodents to their local menus. Perhaps that partially explains why Near Eastern cats, but not European ones, eventually ended up domesticated.
  All six of the Near Eastern cats Krajcarz and her colleagues looked at came from caves in the hills overlooking the fertile lowlands where people farmed. They seem to have ended up in the caves either because that's where they lived and died or because larger predators hauled them home as a snack. People lived in the highlands, in smaller and sparser groups than the farming settlements in the valley below. But there's no evidence that they buried the cats.
 Those caves were 30km to 45km (19 to 28 miles) away from nearby large farming settlements in the valley. That suggests something about these six cats' range, but there's a lot it doesn't tell us. Archaeologists haven't found cat bones at any of the Neolithic settlements in Poland, so there's no evidence yet to tell us whether cats lived and hunted in even closer proximity to people or if some actually lived in human homes or food-storage structures.
title: 古代猫科动物是如何生活在驯化的边缘的
title_en: How ancient cats lived on the brink of domestication

note:
Verizon很不情愿地同意停止投放那些错误地暗示该运营商的5G移动服务在全美都可以使用的广告。
Verizon 5G大量使用毫米波信号，这些信号传播不远，很容易被墙壁和其他障碍物阻挡。最重要的是，5G通常只在很小的地区可以使用，而不是在整个城市都可以使用。然而，更好的商业局委员会(Council Of Better Business Bureau)的国家广告部(NAD)昨日表示，Verizon一直在投放广告，错误地暗示Verizon 5G服务在全国范围内广泛使用。委员会还建议Verizon停止播放两个电视广告，并在未来的广告中避免提出同样的主张。
NAD管理着广告业的自律系统，各公司利用该系统来挑战彼此的广告主张。NAD对Verizon 5G广告的批评异常尖锐。该集团表示，他们建议Verizon停止声称其5G服务在全国各城市广泛使用，并在推出5G服务的城市广泛且容易获得。
NAD表示，Verizon并不否认其目前的5G服务是有限的，而且Verizon的5G覆盖范围主要局限于某些社区的户外地点，而且每个街区的覆盖情况各不相同。NAD还表示，尽管没有证据表明Verizon的速度测试结果准确地描述了Verizon的5G速度，但Verizon的广告吹捧的速度接近2Gbps。
Verizon不情愿地同意停止在广告中做出误导性的声明。NAD公告指出：
在其广告商的声明中，Verizon表示，它将遵守NAD的建议，尽管它并不同意NAD的决定的所有方面。Verizon进一步表示，它仍然致力于自律过程，并坚信客户信息的透明度。
我们询问Verizon它不同意该决定的哪些具体方面，但回复我们的公司发言人没有提供答案。你可以在这里观看看起来令人不快的商业广告，并在运营商的网站上查看Verizon的5G覆盖图。Verizon的发言人告诉我们，电视广告几个月前就停止播放了，但Verizon决定遵守NAD的裁决意味着它将不得不在未来的广告中提出更有限的索赔。
NAD对Verizon不利的裁决是对美国电话电报公司(AT&amp；T)发起的挑战的回应。美国电话电报公司还误导了美国移动用户，将其部分4G网络更名为4G网络。美国电话电报公司从2019年1月开始将许多客户的4G手机网络指示器改为5GE。
在T-Mobile提出质疑后，美国电话电报公司(AT&amp；T)于2020年5月遵守了NAD的建议，停止其5G演进和5G演进，这是5G宣称的第一步，但它显然继续使用具有误导性的5G图标作为手机屏幕上的网络指示器。6月初，美国电话电报公司(AT&amp；T)终于删除了一个鼓吹5GE覆盖实际上是4G的网页，该URL现在重定向到一个根本没有提到5GE的页面。
美国电话电报公司(AT&amp；T)去年也在与威瑞森通讯(Verizon&39；s&34；)5G主张的挑战中取得了部分胜利。Verizon正在对NAD的另一项裁决提出上诉，这是美国电话电报公司(AT&&T)的另一项挑战。这些上诉还会通过广告业的自律系统，在极少数情况下，它们会提交给美国联邦贸易委员会(US Federal Trade Commission)。
在昨天对Verizon不利的裁决中，NAD表示，受到挑战的广告包括Verizon工程师描述Verizon 5G网络超乎寻常的速度和容量的图片，这些工程师站在全美不同的城市，在他们的手机上进行了几次实时速度测试。Verizon没有明确声称覆盖全国，但NAD决定，这些广告暗示消费者将能够访问Verizon。
在一项有代表性的索赔中，Verizon的一则广告称，从曼哈顿中城到丹佛市中心的人们都可以体验Verizon 5G服务。NAD表示，它认为，虽然受到挑战的广告传达了Verizon正在建设5G网络的准确信息，但广告同时兜售了目前的网络性能，最终传达了这样一种净印象，即Verizon超高速5G网络在全国各地的城市都很普遍，而且已经推出，消费者可以广泛而容易地接触到。
此外，NAD发现，这些广告传达了一个信息，即Verizon出众的速度和性能可以在其推出的城市广泛使用。NAD建议Verizon停止声称广告中提到的速度通常是消费者体验的。
NAD公告中的一段总结了Verizon承认其5G网络还远未在全国范围内普及的事实：
关于事实证明，Verizon并不否认其目前的5G服务有限。Verizon的5G覆盖范围主要局限于某些街区的户外地点，每个街区的覆盖范围各不相同。由于Verizon的5G覆盖目前并不能在所有推出5G的城市广泛覆盖，NAD担心这些受到挑战的广告可能会向消费者传达误导性的信息。NAD考虑了Verizon的披露(5G Ultra Wideband)是否只在选定的城市和地点的部分地区提供。支持5G的设备请求的覆盖范围可能有所不同，并不完全适用于所描述的所有地点和场所。)足以限制其索赔，但得出的结论是该索赔不明确且不明显，无法有效地限定或限制索赔。因此，NAD建议停止索赔。
根据NAD多年的指导方针，只有在运营商在全国不同地区提供服务，并且网络覆盖至少2亿人的情况下，运营商才能声称覆盖全国。
NAD昨日表示，看到Verizon误导性5G广告的美国人包括居住在全国各地城镇的人，他们可能在几个月甚至几年内无法获得Verizon的5G覆盖。展望未来，NAD表示，与可用性和性能相关的Verizon 5G广告应该清楚而明显地告诉客户其当前网络的相关实质性限制。
title: 威瑞森的5G网络很小--威瑞森的广告“错误地暗示”它是全国性的
title_en: Verizon’s 5G network is tiny—Verizon ads “falsely implied” it’s nationwide

note:
苹果和爱尔兰就130亿欧元(116亿GB)的税单赢得了对欧盟委员会的上诉。
卢森堡总法院宣布欧盟委员会就爱尔兰税收裁决做出的有利于这家科技巨头的决定无效。
欧盟委员会此前表示，这家科技巨头通过一家总部位于爱尔兰的公司在欧盟各地销售利润，仅支付了1%的有效公司税率。
#EUGeneralCourt撤销@EU_Commission关于爱尔兰#TaxRulings的决定，支持@Apple#Apple#EUCommission#Stateaid pic.twitter.com/KoF6r1n82S。
-欧盟法院(@EUCourtPress)2020年7月15日。
然而，总法院在其判决中指出，委员会宣布苹果国际销售公司(ASI)和苹果欧洲运营公司(AOE)获得了选择性的经济优势，进而获得了国家援助，这是“错误的”。
法院裁定，该委员会没有成功地向“必要的法律标准”表明这是有优势的。
法院在裁决中说：“一般法院认为，欧盟委员会在其主要推理中错误地得出结论，爱尔兰税务当局给予ASI和AOE优势的原因是，没有将ASI和AOE持有的苹果集团知识产权许可证分配给其爱尔兰分支机构，从而将ASI和AOE从苹果集团在北美和南美以外的销售中获得的所有交易收入分配给其爱尔兰分支机构。
“一般法院认为，欧盟委员会在其补贴推理中，没有成功地证明有争议的税收裁决中的方法错误，这些错误将导致ASI和AOE在爱尔兰的应计利润减少。
“虽然总法院对有争议的税务裁决性质不完整和偶尔不一致感到遗憾，但委员会认定的缺陷本身并不足以证明优势的存在。”
欧盟委员会可以就总法院的裁决向欧洲法院提出上诉。上诉必须在接下来的两个月零十天内提出。
131亿欧元存放在托管账户中，这意味着在欧洲法院对欧盟委员会决定的有效性做出最终裁决之前，不能释放收益。
爱尔兰的开放经济基于低企业税和其他吸引跨国公司的激励措施。
在苹果的案例中，这一比例明显低于强加给企业的12.5%的标准。
苹果公司表示，从2003年到2014年，它为在该国产生的利润缴纳了5.77亿美元(5.046亿欧元)的税款，这与爱尔兰的税法一致。
爱尔兰政府反对任何迫使其改变税收做法的努力，这些做法吸引了一些世界领先的金融和科技公司在都柏林设立基地。
爱尔兰财政部在一份声明中表示，对这一判决表示欢迎：“爱尔兰一直明确表示，没有向两家苹果公司--ASI和AOE提供特殊待遇。
“爱尔兰税收的正确数额是按照正常的爱尔兰税收规则征收的。
“爱尔兰对欧盟委员会的决定提出上诉，理由是爱尔兰没有给予国家援助，今天法院的裁决支持了这一观点。”
他说：“欧盟总法院(General Court Of The European Union)实际上裁定维斯塔格专员在做出不利于苹果和爱尔兰的裁决时越界了。
“她未能证明爱尔兰根据当时存在的税收规则给予了选择性待遇或不公平的国家援助。
“这并不是说之前的税收规定令人满意。事实上，自2014年以来，爱尔兰已经堵住了苹果和其他公司用来将这里或任何地方的税额降至最低的漏洞。
“尽管欧盟委员会可能会对判决提出上诉，但现在真正的问题是，欧洲各国政府应同意向全球跨国公司征税制度迈出决定性的一步，尤其是那些数字领域的公司，这些公司可以简单地选择将其盈利的知识产权放在世界的什么地方。”
盈利前人士TD理查德·博伊德·巴雷特(Richard Boyd Barrett)表示，政府“扔掉”了130亿欧元，这是“面对科维德危机时迫切需要的”。
他说：“爱尔兰政府应该为支持苹果公司的上诉和用纳税人的钱帮助阻止爱尔兰从苹果公司获得130亿美元而感到羞愧。
“这笔钱是如此迫切需要，本可以用来缓解新冠肺炎危机，这场危机正对我们的经济和社会产生如此巨大的影响。”
note_en:
Apple and Ireland have won their appeal against the European Commission over a 13 billion euro (£11.6 billion) tax bill.
 The General Court in Luxembourg annulled the decision taken by the commission over Irish tax rulings in favour of the tech giant.
 The commission previously said the tech giant paid an effective corporate tax rate of just 1% on profits from sales made across the EU by routing them through a firm based in Ireland.
   #EUGeneralCourt annuls the decision taken by the  @EU_Commission regarding the Irish  #TaxRulings in favour of  @Apple  #Apple  #EUCommission  #StateAid  pic.twitter.com/KoF6r1n82S
— EU Court of Justice (@EUCourtPress)  July 15, 2020
 However, in its judgment the General Court stated that the commission was “wrong” to declare that Apple Sales International (ASI) and Apple Operations Europe (AOE) had been granted a selective economic advantage and, by extension, state aid.
 The court ruled that the commission did not succeed in showing to the “requisite legal standard” that there was an advantage.
  In its ruling, it said: “The General Court considers that the Commission incorrectly concluded, in its primary line of reasoning, that the Irish tax authorities had granted ASI and AOE an advantage as a result of not having allocated the Apple Group intellectual property licences held by ASI and AOE, and consequently, all of ASI and AOE trading income obtained from the Apple Group’s sales outside North and South America, to their Irish branches.
  “The General Court considers that the Commission did not succeed in demonstrating, in its subsidy line of reasoning, methodological errors in the contested tax rulings which would have led to a reduction in ASI and AOE’s chargeable profits in Ireland.
   “Although the General Court regrets the incomplete and occasionally inconsistent nature of the contested tax rulings, the defects identified by the Commission are not, in themselves, sufficient to prove the existence of an advantage.”
 The Commission can appeal against the General Court’s decision to the European Court of Justice. The appeal must be made within the next two months and ten days.
 The 13.1 billion euro is being held in an escrow account, meaning the proceeds cannot be released until there has been a final determination in the European courts over the validity of the commission’s decision.
 Ireland’s open economy is based on low corporate taxation and other incentives to attract multinationals.
 In Apple’s case, it was significantly below the standard 12.5% imposed on corporations.
 Apple said that from 2003 to 2014, it paid 577 million US dollars (504.6 million euro) in tax on profit generated in the country, in line with the tax laws in Ireland.
 The Irish Government opposes any effort to force it to change its taxation practices, which have tempted some of the world’s leading financial and technology firms to set up base in Dublin.
 In a statement, Ireland’s Department of Finance said it welcomed the judgment: “Ireland has always been clear that there was no special treatment provided to the two Apple companies – ASI and AOE.
 “The correct amount of Irish tax was charged taxation in line with normal Irish taxation rules.
 “Ireland appealed the commission decision on the basis that Ireland granted no state aid, and the decision today from the court supports that view.”
  He said: “The General Court of the European Union has effectively ruled that Commissioner Vestager overreached when she ruled against Apple and Ireland.
 “She failed to prove Ireland granted selective treatment or unfair state aid in relation to the tax rules that existed at the time.
  “This is not to say the previous tax rules were satisfactory. In fact, since 2014, Ireland has closed the loophole that Apple and other corporations used to minimise the amount of tax they paid here or anywhere.
 “While the Commission may appeal the judgement, the real issue now is for European governments to agree a decisive step forward to reform the global system for taxing multinationals, especially those in the digital sector that can simply choose where in the world to locate their profit-making intellectual property rights.”
 People Before Profit TD Richard Boyd Barrett said the Government has “thrown away” 13 billion euro which is “desperately needed in the face of the Covid crisis”.
 He said: “The Irish government should hang their heads in shame for supporting Apple in their appeal and using taxpayers’ money to help prevent Ireland receiving 13 billion from Apple.
 “That money, which is so desperately needed, could have been put to use in mitigating the Covid-19 crisis which is having such a massive impact on our economy and society.”
title: 苹果赢得130亿欧元税收上诉，政府应低头羞愧
title_en: Govt should hang 'heads in shame' as Apple wins 13 billion euro tax appeal

现在，我们都生活在一种一切都被颠覆的气候中。会议是虚拟的，未来的经济和世界的集体健康是未知的，作为投资者-或创始人-会有全新的参与参数和规则。
我们和格林坐下来谈了一个小时，谈到了所有这一切的挑战，再加上达成交易，经营企业，突然发现你安静的B2B名字正在变成一个动词。这是一次有趣的对话，对于企业初创企业和投资者来说值得一读，但-类似于B2B如何溢出到消费者-对更多的人来说也同样有洞察力。
Extra Crunch Live是我们面向额外Crunch会员的新虚拟扬声器系列。人们可以在聊天过程中现场提问，嘉宾包括艾琳·李(Aileen Lee)、柯尔斯滕·格林(Kirsten Green)、马克·库班(Mark Cube)等等。您可以在这里查看日程表。
下面，你会看到我们最近与格林谈话的一份略有编辑的文字记录。
采购也没有太大不同。我们遵循相同的尽职调查流程，所以当我们进行投资时，整个团队基本上都会潜心工作，进行尽职调查。因此，我们提供经理推荐人和客户电话，并花时间与每个管理团队进行一对一的交流。在某些方面，它是更好的。首先，我们可以很容易地与每个管理团队成员进行分组讨论，然后再回来。所以这次会议充满了活力，这是我们以前从未有过的。我们基本上可以录制下来，并与不能参加的人分享。所以当我们一起做决定的时候，我们都掌握了所有的信息。实际上，那是相当特别的。所以它花的时间稍微长了一点，可能比我们在其他情况下多花了大约50%的时间。但我认为，实际上，现在知道我们做了什么，我们可能可以把它压缩回我们正常的时间框架。所以我认为在很多方面，我们已经像很多人一样学到了我们可以远程做我们以前认为不可能做的事情。希望我们能看到这笔投资的结果，但我们对此超级兴奋。
你是否在考虑更多硅谷以外的初创企业，他们如何看待硅谷以外的地方？
note_en:
Now, we’re all living in a climate where everything has been turned upside down. Meetings are virtual, the future economy and collective health of the world are unknowns, and being an investor — or a founder — comes with completely new parameters and rules of engagement.
 We sat down with Green for an enlightening hour to talk about the challenges of all that, plus making deals, running a business, and suddenly finding your quiet, B2B name being turned into a verb. It was an interesting conversation, worth a read for enterprise startups and investors, but — similar to how B2B can spill into consumer — equally insightful for many more.
 Extra Crunch Live is our new virtual speaker series for Extra Crunch members. Folks can ask their own questions live during the chat, with guests that include  Aileen Lee, Kirsten Green, Mark Cuban and many, many more. You can check out the schedule  here.
 Below, you’ll find a lightly edited transcript of our recent chat with Green.
  Sourcing is not much different. We follow the same due diligence process, so when we make an investment, the whole team basically dives in and does due diligence. So we make manager references and customer calls and spend time with each of the management team having one-on-ones. In some ways, it was better. First of all, we could very easily do breakout rooms with each of the individual management team members and then come back. So there was this dynamism to the meeting that we hadn’t had before. We were able to basically record it and share it with folks that couldn’t participate. So all of us had all the information when we were making the decision together. That was pretty special, actually. So it took a little bit longer, it probably took about 50% longer than we would have done otherwise. But I think actually, now knowing what we’ve done, we could probably compress it back to our normal timeframe. So I think in a lot of ways, we’ve learned like a lot of folks that we can do things remotely that we probably didn’t think were possible before. Hopefully, we’ll see how the investment turns out, but we’re super excited about it.
 Are you considering more startups outside the Valley, and how are they viewing their own place outside the Valley?
title: Emerging的杰森·格林认为，一些技术反弹是合理的，但B2B的机会仍然大于挑战
title_en: Emergence’s Jason Green thinks some of the tech backlash is justified, but the B2B opportunities still outweigh the challenges

音频简报在某种程度上是Alexa的Flash简报的竞争对手，后者已经成为赶上头条新闻的流行方式。但在苹果的案例中，简报会是由人主持的，而不是虚拟助理。苹果新闻编辑兼联合主持人舒米塔·巴苏(Shumita Basu)和杜阿尔特·杰拉尔迪诺(Duarte Geraldino)将引导听众浏览当天的头条新闻。然后，他们将在简报的其余部分更深入地讨论大约三四篇文章。
简报会总共将持续大约7到8分钟，美国所有的苹果新闻读者都可以看到。
每周一到周五都会有新的简报到达，可以在美国的Apple News应用程序、苹果播客中播放，也可以在包括Mac和HomePod在内的苹果设备上使用Siri语音命令启动。
然而，另一个新的音频功能，音频故事，只对Apple News+的订阅者开放。
从今天开始，苹果新闻每周将制作约20个新的音频故事，涉及一系列主题，由配音演员讲述。这些故事不是原创内容，而是由Essence、Esquire、Fast Company、GQ、New York Magazine、Sports Illustrated、Time、Vanity Fair、Vogue、Wire等报纸以及华尔街日报和洛杉矶时报等报纸发表的专题报道和其他长篇文章的专业叙事版本。
几年前，苹果曾与SpokenLayer合作，将音频旁白带到新闻中，但那次努力的重点是增强苹果播客的体验。该公司表示，它没有与SpokenLayer合作开发苹果新闻的新功能，而是与语音人才自己合作，在自己的录音棚里录制这些音频故事。苹果拒绝透露有多少人被聘请为苹果新闻(Apple News)从事音频节目的工作，但确实证实了该公司扩大了专门负责音频的员工人数。
音频故事将只向美国的订阅者提供。苹果没有说明是否计划将这款产品扩展到国际市场。
为了给新的音频功能腾出空间，苹果新闻应用程序的导航体验进行了重新设计。现在，在新闻应用程序的底部，在屏幕底部的“News+”和“Follow”选项卡之间，有一个“音频”选项卡将用户引导到新内容。点击后，用户将首先在屏幕顶部看到今日苹果新闻的最新一集，然后是您添加到UP NEXT队列中的新音频故事。下面是一组个性化的推荐，告诉你下一步要听什么。
虽然标签组织了新的音频节目，但当用户在应用程序的其他地方浏览时，也会被介绍给音频。例如，当您在“今日”标签中滚动新闻故事或阅读“新闻+源”时，一个新的音频“新闻+”徽章将出现在有旁白的故事旁边。然后，您可以单击该故事开始旁白，而无需更改屏幕。该功能还可以让你在阅读和听力之间来回转换，因为它会记住你在一篇较长的文章中读到的地方，并在你返回时从那里开始。
与这些努力相关的是，CarPlay将支持新闻应用程序，这样用户就可以在开车的同时收听这些音频故事和苹果今日新闻简报。当你在iPhone和汽车之间来回移动时，不同设备之间的收听进度也会同步。
虽然今天的大新闻是苹果新闻即将播出的音频节目，但这款应用程序今天也将引入更多的本地报道。
在选定的市场-旧金山和更广泛的旧金山湾区、纽约、洛杉矶和休斯顿-苹果新闻将以由当地编辑领导的精心策划的本地新闻体验为特色。这些版块将包括更广泛的故事，涉及当地天气、政治、体育、餐饮和餐馆以及其他新闻，并将为每个读者提供个性化的信息。苹果新闻的竞争对手Flipboard最近推出了一个类似的功能，以利用对本地新闻日益增长的需求，特别是在冠状病毒大流行的情况下，读者需要访问他们所在的城市和州关于新冠肺炎对他们日常生活影响的报道。
Apple News+今天还扩大了当地和地区性报纸的选择范围，现在可以访问“夏洛特观察家报”、“爱达荷州政治家报”、“堪萨斯城星报”、“迈阿密先驱报”、“新闻与放大器观察家报”(北卡罗来纳州罗利)和美国“州报”(南卡罗来纳州哥伦比亚)。在加拿大，它增加了法语报纸“世界报”，并将于今年夏天晚些时候推出“环球邮报”。
虽然苹果新闻平台目前在美国、英国、澳大利亚和加拿大的月度活跃用户超过1.25亿，但据报道，新闻+订阅的吸收在扩大付费订户基础方面遇到了困难。据彭博社报道，今年早些时候，负责管理与出版商和广告商关系的苹果新闻服务业务主管利兹·施梅尔(Liz Schimel)在订阅服务起步缓慢后辞职。
参与Apple News+的出版商将100%保留其销售广告的收入，并可以参与苹果销售的回填广告，保留70%的收入。他们还可以出售自己的订阅，并根据参与度获得Apple News+收入的分成。
但并不是所有的出版商都认为Apple News+是增长业务的最佳方式。例如，“纽约时报”(New York Times)在6月份宣布，将不再分发苹果新闻应用程序中的文章。
尽管有这些报道，苹果公司表示对Apple News+的吸引力感到满意。但今天的新功能显然是为了刺激每月9.99美元的付费订阅的增长。
苹果新闻的新功能将通过软件更新(iOS13.6、iPadOS13.6或MacOS10.15.6)的方式于今天发布。
note_en:
The audio briefing is somewhat of a competitor to Alexa’s Flash Briefing, which has become a popular way to catch up on the top news headlines. But in Apple’s case, the briefing is hosted by people, not a virtual assistant. Apple News editors and co-hosts, Shumita Basu and Duarte Geraldino, will guide listeners through the day’s headlines. They will then spend the remainder of the briefing discussing around three or four articles in a more in-depth fashion.
   In total, the briefing will run for roughly seven to eight minutes in length and will be accessible to all Apple News readers in the U.S.
 A new briefing will arrive every Monday through Friday, and can be played in the Apple News app in the U.S., in Apple Podcasts, or it can be launched using Siri voice commands across Apple devices, including Mac and HomePod.
 Another new audio feature, audio stories, is only being made available to Apple News+ subscribers, however.
 Starting today, Apple News will produce around 20 new audio stories per week across a range of topics, which are narrated by voice actors. These stories aren’t original content, but will instead be professionally-narrated versions of feature reporting and other long-form pieces published by Essence, Esquire, Fast Company, GQ, New York Magazine, Sports Illustrated, TIME, Vanity Fair, Vogue, Wired, and others, as well as newspapers like  The Wall Street Journal and the Los Angeles Times.
 Apple several years ago had  partnered with SpokenLayer to bring audio narration to the news, but that effort was focused on enhancing the Apple Podcasts experience. The company says it’s not partnered with SpokenLayer on the new feature for Apple News, but is instead working with voice talent itself and recording these audio stories in its own studios. Apple declined to say how many have been hired to work on audio programming for Apple News, but did confirm it expanded headcount specifically for audio.
 The audio stories will be made available to subscribers in the U.S. only. Apple didn’t say if it’s planning to expand the offering to international markets.
 To make room for the new audio features, the Apple News app navigation experience has been redesigned. There’s now an “Audio” tab directing users to the new content at the bottom of the news application, in between the “News+” and “Following” tabs at the bottom of the screen. When tapped, users will first see the latest episode of Apple News Today at the top of the screen, following by the new Audio Stories that you’ve added to your Up Next queue. Below that will be a set of personalized recommendations of what to listen to next.
 While the tab organizes the new audio programming, users will also be introduced to audio as they’re browsing elsewhere in the app. For example, as you’re scrolling through news stories in the Today tab or reading News+ feeds, a new audio News+ badge will appear next to stories that have narration. You can then click that story to begin the narration without having to change screens. The feature will also allow you to transition back and forth between reading and listening as it will remember where you left off in a longer piece, and begin there when you return.
 Related to these efforts, CarPlay will support the News app so users can listen to these audio stories and the Apple News Today briefing while driving. Listening progress will also sync between devices, as you move from iPhone to car and back again.
  While the big news today is the audio programming coming to Apple News, the app is also today introducing expanded local coverage.
 In select markets — San Francisco and the wider Bay Area, New York, L.A., and Houston — Apple News will feature a curated local news experience headed by a local editor. These sections will include a wider range of stories across areas like local weather, politics, sports, dining and restaurants, and other news and will be personalized to each reader. Apple News rival Flipboard  recently launched a similar feature to capitalize on the growing demand for local news, particularly amid the coronavirus pandemic where readers need access to their city and state’s reports of the impact of COVID-19 on their daily lives.
  Apple News+ also today expanded its selection of local and regional newspapers to now offer access to The Charlotte Observer, The Idaho Statesman, The Kansas City Star, the Miami Herald, The News & Observer (Raleigh, NC), and The State (Columbia, SC), in the U.S. In Canada it added French-language newspaper Le Devoir and will bring on The Globe and Mail later this summer.
 While the Apple News platform currently reaches over 125 million monthly active users in the U.S., U.K. Australia, and Canada, the uptake on the News+ subscription has  reportedly seen struggles in terms of growing its paid subscriber base. Earlier this year, Apple’s News service business chief, Liz Schimel, who managed relationship with publishers and advertisers, stepped down after the subscription service’s slow start,  Bloomberg reported.
 Publishers participating in Apple News+ keep 100% of the revenue from ads they sell and can participate in backfill ads that Apple sells,  keeping 70% of the revenue. They can also sell their own subscription and receive a cut of Apple News+ revenue, based on engagement.
 But not all publishers believe Apple News+ is the best way to grow their business. The New York Times, for example, announced in June it will  no longer distribute articles in the Apple News app.
 Despite the reports, Apple says it’s pleased with Apple News+ traction. But today’s new features are clearly designed to spark growth for the $9.99 per month paid  subscription.
 The new Apple News features will come by way of software updates ( iOS 13.6, iPadOS 13.6, or macOS 10.15.6), arriving today.

星链服务将需要使用专门的硬件来接收和传输数据到星链星座和从星链星座发送数据，并且需要“清晰地看到北方天空”才能发挥作用。上图所示的硬件是一个小型卫星天线，SpaceX首席执行官兼创始人埃隆·马斯克此前曾将其描述为“棍子上的不明飞行物”。你可以在上面的图片中看到它，它看起来与典型的卫星碟形天线没有什么不同，尽管很难从在Starlink服务器上发现的图像来判断它的规模。
StarLink将免费向测试版测试员提供此硬件，他们将负责自己安装。这些套件包括碟子的电源，以及安装硬件，这些硬件旨在根据人们的住所满足他们的特定需求。该网站特别指示Beta测试员在安装他们的工具包时不要雇佣外部帮助。FAQ指出，如果没有清晰的天空视野，用户将无法获得良好的连接。
许多现有的卫星互联网服务提供商在使用点使用碟形天线来实现连接，但他们通常与安装商合作为用户设置连接。SpaceX可能也会在测试期之后走这条路，但它显然希望在发射前的测试期限制访问。
根据该公司在FAQ中的说法，在正确连接的情况下，StarLink的服务质量应该是“高”的，但它也“不会保持一致”。这是因为SpaceX将在整个测试计划中执行远程软件更新和其他网络优化，根据网站文本，这意味着它不适合作为“游戏或工作目的”的解决方案。
StarLink还指出，它将在测试期间监控网络上的所有活动，并特别禁止“非法活动”，如下载或存储盗版材料，并保留基于任何此类活动“暂停或终止”测试者参与的权利。
用户可以随时取消他们的参与，并鼓励他们只有在能够安全地访问适合这样做的区域时才安装硬件(所以，如果你住在公寓这样的共享住宅中，这可能会限制你参与的能力)。
根据Starlink的说法，Beta测试者被要求对他们参与的细节保密，包括网络速度和质量等。预计他们每天还会花30到1个小时的时间来“测试星联服务，并定期提供反馈”，反馈通过“调查、电话、电子邮件和其他方式”传递。
StarLink还指定参与者准备好在测试期结束时(按StarLink的运费)或在要求的任何时候退还他们的Starlink套件。该公司还表示，为了“测试SpaceX的订购和计费系统”，它将需要信用卡或借记卡信息，并将收取“象征性的费用”(这笔费用似乎尚未敲定，但在安装时将从1美元到3美元不等，然后每月重复收取)，但它特别指出，在测试期间，实际的Starlink服务和租借硬件都是免费提供的。
StarLink给潜在的测试版测试者发了一封电子邮件，说他们预计私人测试版将在今年夏天开始，所以看到他们已经准备好了资产和内容来启动和运行它，以供被邀请参加的人使用也就不足为奇了。如果一切顺利，该公司希望将其扩大到开放测试版，最初覆盖美国北部和加拿大，并在明年额外发射卫星后将其扩展到更多地区。
note_en:
Starlink service will require use of specialized hardware for reception and transmission of data to and from the Starlink constellation, as well as “a clear view of the Northern sky” to function. The hardware, pictured above, is a small satellite dish that  SpaceX   CEO and founder  Elon Musk   has previously described as a “UFO on a stick.” You can see it pictured above, and it doesn’t look at that different from a typical satellite dish, though it’s hard to judge at scale from the images found on the Starlink servers.
 Starlink will be providing this hardware to beta testers free of charge, and they’ll be responsible for installing it themselves. The kits include a power supply for the dish, along with mounting hardware designed to work with a person’s specific needs based on their dwelling. The website specifically instructs beta testers not to employ outside help when mounting their kit. Without a clear view of the sky, the FAQ notes, users won’t be able to get a good connection.
  Many existing satellite internet service providers use dishes at point-of-use to enable the connection, but they typically work with installers to set it up for users. SpaceX might also go that route following the beta period, but it clearly wants to limit access during this pre-launch testing period.
   Starlink service quality should be “high” when properly connected, but it also “will not be consistent” according to the company in the FAQ. That’s because SpaceX will be performing remote software updates and other network optimizations throughout the testing program, meaning it won’t be suitably as a solution for “gaming or work purposes,” according to the site text.
 Starlink also notes that it will be monitoring all activity on the network during the testing period and forbids specifically “illegal activities” such as downloading or storing pirated materials, and that it reservers the right to “suspend or terminate” beta tester participation based on any such activity.
 Users are allowed to cancel their participation at any time, and are encouraged to only install the hardware if they’re able to safely access an area suitable for doing so (so, if you’re in a shared dwelling like an apartment, that may limit your ability to take part).
  Beta testers are required to keep the details of their participation confidential, including things like network speed and quality, according to Starlink. They’re also expected to dedicate between 30 minus and an hour per day to “testing the Starlink Services and providing feedback on a periodic basis,” with feedback delivered via “surveys, phone calls, emails and other means.”
  Starlink also specifies that participants be ready to send back their Starlink kits at the end of the beta period (at Starlink’s shipping cost), or at any time that it’s requested. The company also says that it will require credit card or debit information and will charge a “nominal fee” (which doesn’t seem finalized, but would range from $1 to $3 at setup, and then recurring monthly) in order to “test SpaceX’s ordering and billing systems,” but it notes specifically that the actual Starlink service is provided free, as well as the loaner hardware, for the duration of the test.
 Starlink emailed potential beta testers saying to expect the private beta to kick off this summer, so it’s not surprising to see they already have assets and content in place to get it up and running for those invited to participate. If all goes well, the company hopes to expand that to an open beta, with initial coverage in the northern U.S. and Canada, and expand that to more geographies next year after additional satellite launches.
title: 泄露披露了SpaceX的StarLink互联网服务测试版计划的细节
title_en: Leak reveals details of SpaceX’s Starlink internet service beta program

note:
警方消息人士向ABC新闻证实，周二，一名33岁的科技企业家被发现在曼哈顿的公寓里被斩首并肢解。
警方周三确认受害者为法希姆·萨利赫(Fahim Saleh)，并表示这起死亡事件被认为是他杀。目前还没有逮捕任何人，调查仍在进行中。
萨利赫与人共同创立了拼车应用Pathao，这款应用在班加尔达什很受欢迎。最近，他是Gokada的首席执行官，Gokada是一家总部位于尼日利亚的摩托车拼车和送货公司，该公司面临财务挫折，最近解雇了大部分员工。
根据周一的监控视频，萨利赫在下午1点40分左右被看到。一名男子戴着西装、手套、帽子和口罩，提着公文包，紧随其后进入了他的公寓电梯。当电梯到达七楼，直通萨利赫的公寓时，他立即倒下，这显然是一次袭击的结果。
萨利赫的身体是周二下午被他的妹妹发现的，她在一天没有收到萨利赫的消息后非常担心。警方消息人士称，在监控录像中可以看到她进入大楼，但没有看到嫌疑杀手离开，这让执法部门认为她可能中断了肢解行为。据消息人士透露，还有第二条路可以从公寓出去，通过一个服务入口。
消息人士称，萨利赫的躯干被从他的头部和四肢上分离出来，这是在附近的几个大袋子里发现的。据执法部门消息人士称，还发现了一把电锯，仍插在电源插座上。他的狗在公寓里被发现还活着。
Gokada在推特上确认了其创始人兼首席执行官突然而悲惨的逝世，称他是伟大的领导者、鼓舞人心的人物，是我们所有人的积极光芒。
该公司表示，我们的心与他的朋友、家人以及所有那些感受到我们正在经历的痛苦和心碎的人在一起，就在Gokada这里，我们的心与他的朋友、家人和所有那些感受到我们目前正在经历的痛苦和心碎的人联系在一起。法希姆对我们的远见和信念将永远与我们同在，我们会非常想念他。感谢您在我们经历这一切的过程中理解我们。
note_en:
A 33-year-old tech entrepreneur was discovered decapitated and dismembered in his Manhattan apartment on Tuesday, police sources confirmed to ABC News.
 Police identified the victim as Fahim Saleh on Wednesday and said the death was deemed a homicide. No arrests have been made and the investigation remains ongoing.
   Saleh co-founded Pathao, a ride-share app that's popular in Bangledash. More recently, he was the CEO of Gokada, a motorcycle ride-sharing and delivery company based  Nigeria, which has faced financial setbacks and recently laid off most of its staff.
 According to surveillance video from Monday, Saleh was seen around 1:40 p.m. being followed into his apartment elevator by a man wearing a suit, gloves, hat and mask and carrying a briefcase. When the elevator arrives at the seventh floor, which opened right into Saleh's apartment, he falls immediately, the apparent result of an attack.
 Saleh's body was found Tuesday afternoon by his sister who was concerned after she had not heard from him for a day. She can be seen in surveillance footage entering the building, but the suspected killer is not seen leaving, leading law enforcement to believe she may have interrupted the act of dismembering, police sources said. There is a second way out of the apartment, through a service entrance, according to the sources.
  Saleh's torso was detached from his head and limbs, which were found nearby in several large bags, sources said. An electric saw was also recovered, still plugged into an electrical outlet, according to law enforcement sources. His dog was found alive in the apartment.
   Gokada confirmed the "sudden and tragic loss" of its founder and CEO via Twitter, calling him "a great leader, inspiration and positive light for all of us."
 "Our hearts go out to his friends, family and all those feeling the pain and heartbreak we are currently experiencing, here at Gokada," the company said. "Fahim's vision and belief in us will be with us forever, and we will miss him dearly. Thank you for understanding as we get through this."
title: 科技公司首席执行官在纽约的公寓里被发现被斩首和肢解
title_en: Tech CEO Found Decapitated and Dismembered In His NYC Apartment

在过去的几个月里，我们一直在为Substack上的独立作家试行一项法律支持计划。作为该计划的一部分，我们一直与一流的媒体律师合作，为因工作而面临法律不确定性或压力的作家提供免费的建议和指导。这种支持包括对个别故事的出版前法律审查和对停止和停止函的答复。从今天起，我们将更广泛地提供这一法律支持计划。
子堆栈的优势之一是它帮助编写者建立独立性。当读者通过付费订阅直接支持作家时，这些作家就可以自由地从事他们认为重要的工作。通过这种模式，独立作家也可以找到财务稳定性--Substack上排名靠前的作家每年赚数十万美元--但我们知道，钱只是方程式的一部分。
重要的写作让有权势的人承担责任-而且通常情况下，这是有权势的人宁愿不支持的安排。在某些情况下，敌手使用法律行动的威胁，试图停止让他们感到不舒服的工作。例如，最近，代表一名政客的一名有权势的律师威胁一名副堆栈作家，因为他报道了这名议员可疑的商业关系。当作者在我们支持计划的律师的支持下坚持自己的立场时，这些威胁就消失了。在SubStack，我们想清楚地表明，任何使用这种恐吓策略的人也将不得不与我们算账。我们将利用我们的财政和法律资源，强烈反对任何劝阻堆叠下层作者工作的不诚实行为。
我们邀请有兴趣的作者通过我们的在线申请表申请Substack Defender计划。
对于该计划的第一次迭代，应用程序向那些启用付费订阅并发布可能会招致不合理法律压力的美国作家开放，例如滥用版权法、攻击第一修正案权利和虚假诽谤索赔。在接受该计划后，编写者可以使用单独的表格来请求特定案例的帮助。子堆栈将最终选择谁被接受加入计划，以及支持哪些案例。一旦案件由项目的律师接手，Substack将根据我们的判断，支付最高100万美元的费用(在特殊情况下，我们可能会支付更多费用)。
SubStack Defender是众多计划中的一个，这些计划将形成对独立编写者的全面支持结构。我们正在建立一个建立在信任基础上的新媒体生态系统，在这个生态系统中，作家拥有自己与读者的关系，读者有权对他们的信息饮食做出深思熟虑的决定。然而，要实现这些目标，仅靠软件是不够的。我们致力于创造条件，让独立作家蓬勃发展，这意味着帮助恢复因继续拆除曾经支持健康新闻自由的模式和机构而受到侵蚀的基础设施。
我们将在一个服务项目上进行大笔投资，该项目包括与医疗保健、个人理财、编辑、分发、设计和协同工作空间相关的倡议。在我们看来，独立并不意味着孤独。
这些为编写器构建服务的努力将是Substack持续运营的核心部分。我们将不断改进这方面的工作，欢迎对潜在合作的反馈和询问。
感谢到目前为止支持SubStack编写器的每一个人，感谢他们帮助我们构建一个更美好的写作未来。
note_en:
For the last few months, we have been piloting a legal support program for independent writers on Substack. As part of this program, we have been working with first-rate media lawyers to provide free advice and direction to writers who are facing legal uncertainty or pressure because of their work. This support has included pre-publication legal review of individual stories and responses to cease-and-desist letters. As of today, we’re making this legal support program more widely available.
 One of the advantages of Substack is that it helps writers build independence. When readers support writers directly with paid subscriptions, those writers are free to pursue the work they believe is important. Through this model, independent writers can also find financial stability – the top writers on Substack are making hundreds of thousands of dollars a year – but we know that money is only part of the equation.
 Important writing holds the powerful to account – and quite often, that’s an arrangement that the powerful would rather not support. In some cases, antagonists use threats of legal action in an attempt to stop the work that makes them uncomfortable. Recently, for instance, a high-powered lawyer representing a politician threatened a Substack writer for his coverage of the lawmaker’s questionable business ties. The threats disappeared when the writer, backed by our support program’s lawyers, stood his ground. At Substack, we want to make it crystal clear that anyone who uses such intimidation tactics will also have to reckon with us. We will use our financial and legal resources to vigorously oppose any bad-faith efforts to dissuade Substack writers from doing their work.
 We invite interested writers to apply for the Substack Defender program via our   online application form.
 For this first iteration of the program, applications are open to US-based writers who have enabled paid subscriptions and publish work that may attract unreasonable legal pressure, such as abuses of copyright laws, assaults on first amendment rights, and spurious defamation claims. Upon acceptance to the program, writers can use a separate form to request help for specific cases. Substack will make the ultimate choice on who is accepted into the program and which cases to support. Once a case has been taken on by the program’s lawyers, Substack, at our discretion, will cover fees up to $1 million (in exceptional cases, we may cover even more).
 Substack Defender is one of many initiatives that will form a comprehensive support structure for independent writers. We are building a new media ecosystem based on trust, where writers own their relationships with readers, and readers have the power to make considered decisions about their information diet. To realize these objectives, however, software alone is not enough. We are committed to creating the conditions that allow independent writers to flourish, which means helping to restore the infrastructure that has been eroded by the continued dismantling of models and institutions that once supported a healthy free press.
 We will make a large investment in a services program that includes initiatives related to healthcare, personal finance, editing, distribution, design, and coworking spaces. In our view, being independent shouldn’t mean being alone.
 These efforts to build services for writers will be a central part of Substack’s operations on an ongoing basis. We will continually improve this aspect of our work and welcome feedback and inquiries about potential cooperation.
 Thank you to everyone who has supported Substack writers so far, and for helping us build a better future for writing.
title: Substack开始向付费订阅的美国作家提供法律支持，这些作家发表的作品可能会招致不合理的法律压力
title_en: Substack begins offering legal support to US-based writers with paid subscriptions who publish work that may attract unreasonable legal pressure
